Yee Advanced Orthopedics & Sports Appointment: 03/24/2026 8:00 AM Medicine, PLLC WARM SPRINGS Provider: Morris, Gary Patient Information Name: DORIS J WATKINS Home Phone: (702) 302-4467 D.O.B: 05/12/1945 Work Phone: Sex: Female Mobile Phone: (702) 278-1948 SSN: XXX-XX-2652 Email: CWCWBA@AOL.COM MRN: 77034 Preferred Contact Method: Unspecified PMS ID: 77034 Language: English Marital Status: Married Emergency Contact: Celester watkins Race: Black or African American Emergency Contact Phone: 702-278-1965 Ethnicity: Unknown Employer: Address: 1000 MEZPAH ST LAS VEGAS, NV 89106 Primary Care Provider: CHAMIAN, NOEL Referring Providers: Baumgartl, William Primary Insurance Information Carrier: Medicare of Nevada Address: PO BOX 6776 FARGO, ND 581086776 Policy #: 1XJ5N81TM55 Group ID/Name: Phone Number: Secondary Insurance Information Carrier: LIFESHIELD NATIONAL SUPP Address: PO BOX 211116 EGAN, MN 55121 Policy #: W00002355S Group ID/Name: Phone Number: AM ADVANCED WATKINS, DORIS Uports Visit Note - March 5, 2026 PMS ID: Sex: DOB: Phone: MRN: 77034 Female 05/12/1945 (702) 302-4467 77034 Allergies Penicillins Chief Complaints: hydrocodone 1. L KNEE aspirin codeine amlodipine dyclonine HPI: This is an 80 year old female who: nitrofurantoin 1. is being seen for a chief complaint of L KNEE. PT COMES IN TODAY WITH LEFT KNEE PAIN. STATES IT HAS BEEN ONGOING FOR A WHILE. SHE HAD MRI DONE AT DES RAD, AND X RAYS DONE IN THE OFFICE. Medical History Musculoskeletal Exam: History Examination today reveals crepitus and pain throughout range of motion left knee. Mild valgus deformity. Calf is soft Carpal tunnel syndrome and nontender. Stable ligamentous examination. Well-healed arthroscopic portals Chronic low back pain Osteoarthritis Primary gout Right handed Impression/Plan: 1. Knee Arthritis, Left Musculoskeletal Unilateral primary osteoarthritis, left knee (M17.12) Family History located on the left knee. Diabetes Plan: Additional Notes. Hypertension Osteoarthritis Patient Specific Notes: The patient has osteoarthritis left knee. She has been treated conservatively as well as Scoliosis with an arthroscopic debridement. She says she is ready for a total knee replacement. She did have an MRI scan of the left knee which shows lateral meniscus pathology as well as lateral compartment arthritis. She will need preoperative medical clearance. Follow-up for final surgical decision making visit Musculoskeletal Surgery History of arthroscopy of knee joint Repair of meniscus Plan: Order Surgery: Knee Arthroplasty. SURGERY INFORMATION: Social History Single Question Alcohol Screening: 0 Provider: Gary Morris days Caffeine Use: Other Priority: normal Exercise: Once a day EtOH none Smoking status Unspecified Estimated Length of Procedure: 90 minutes Additional Details: Not any Diagnosis: Knee Arthritis, Left - M17.12 Surgical Site: left knee Shoe Size Position: Supine Shoe size: 8 1/2 Plan: Counseling - Knee DJD. Musculoskeletal care: I advised the patient that most patients with degenerative joint disease of the knee can Tobacco Use achieve some measure of pain relief from nonsurgical options like medications, lifestyle modification, exercise and Does not use vaping products Does not use smokeless tobacco physical therapy, and use of supportive devices or change in footwear. The importance of maintaining an ideal Tobacco Product: None body weight or losing weight to reduce stress on the knee joint is a cornestone of nonoperative management. Pneumonia vaccination not Expectations: I counseled the patient about the natural history of degenerative joint disease of the knee which administered or previously received typically has exacerbations and remissions. Many people report that changes in the weather also affect the degree of pain from arthritis. The knee joint does not heal on its own when there is arthritis. With time, the symptoms of arthritis may get worse. For some, conservative management may not be not satisfactory. These Advance Care individuals should consider surgical options. Healthcare Proxy: No Contact Office if your knee pain worsens, or if the knee becomes warm or accompanied by a fever. Living Will: No Full Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation Follow up. Other Instructions: AFTER SURGICAL DECISION MAKING Staff: Gary Morris (Primary Provider) (Bill Under) Tracey Demartino Electronically Signed By: Gary Morris, 03/05/2026 12:01 PM PST Gary Morris (Primary Provider) (Bill Under) SAHARA Page 1 (702) 740-5327 Work 8230 W Sahara Ave Ste 111 Las Vegas, NV 89117-8930
